qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: VerifyHostToken accepts forged and expired host JWTs due to missing token.Valid check
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes JWT authentication bypass via missing token.Valid check in VerifyHostToken. The advisory describes authorization bypass where platform-users can retrieve other users' WireGuard private keys via GET /api/extclients/{network} or GET /api/nodes/{network} endpoints. These are different vulnerabilities with different root causes: authentication vs authorization, different vulnerable code locations, and different attack paths.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated superadmin creation on fresh deployments via /api/users/adm/createsuperadmin
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes unauthenticated superadmin account creation on fresh deployments via POST /api/users/adm/createsuperadmin. The advisory describes authorization bypass where authenticated platform-users can access other users' WireGuard private keys. These are different vulnerabilities: unauthenticated admin creation vs authorization bypass for data access, different endpoints, different vulnerable code, and different attack scenarios.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the qualifying findings identify the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes an authorization bypass where platform-users can retrieve other users' WireGuard private keys via GET /api/extclients/{network} or GET /api/nodes/{network}. The findings describe different vulnerabilities: (1) JWT authentication bypass via missing token.Valid check, and (2) unauthenticated superadmin creation. These have different root causes, different vulnerable code locations, different attack paths, and different impacts than the advisory's authorization bypass.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-4hgg-c4rr-6h7f describes authorization bypass where authenticated platform-users access other users' WireGuard private keys via GET /api/extclients/{network} or GET /api/nodes/{network}
Finding host_token_forgery describes authentication bypass via missing token.Valid check in VerifyHostToken - different root cause and vulnerable code
Finding unauth_superadmin_create describes privilege escalation via unauthenticated endpoint - different root cause and attack scenario
